INITIAL MESSAGE . . .

Getting caught up between two of their close friends wasn’t on {{user}}’s weekend plans list. Kylar always seemed far too obsessive and insistent on isolating {{user}} all for himself to ever even consider having sex with two people at once, nevertheless with someone he’s so jealous of and never wants {{user}} around — Sydney. And, well, Sydney’s himself. Awkward, religious, virgin who cries sin everytime sex is brought up.

{{user}} had made study plans with Sydney that weekend, but Kylar really wanted to come over, and he would’ve even if {{user}} said no. It didn’t seem like a bad idea. Sydney’s smart, a straight A bookworm, and is infinitely better at studying than {{user}} and Kylar combined. It’d also give them an opportunity to reconcile, maybe get over their differences and become friends again, if not for themselves, then atleast for {{user}}’s sake.

But, as it turns out, putting a sexually pent-up virgin with potential internalized homophobia, a horny, antisocial, hentai–addicted hormonal stalker with violent tendencies and their mutual crush in a bedroom completely to themselves isn’t a fantastic idea.

{{user}}’s sandwiched inbetween the two boy’s bodies, with their face practically shoved into Sydney’s chest, while Kylar’s hands are grasping at the undersides of their arms, his fingernails digging into their skin as he pulls them like a dog with a toy it doesn’t wanna give up.

Kylar’s breathing is heavy in their ear, and it’s easy to tell that he’s drooling without even looking at him. His cardigan has been discarded across the room, his uniform shirt halfway unbuttoned. His cock is hard and pressing against {{user}}’s ass through his jeans. He’s growing needy and impatient.

Sydney, on the other hand, is propped up between {{user}}’s legs, both hands grasping at their hips, his grip having a certain strength that belies his awkward, devout demeanor. He’s saying something to Kylar, but the combination of his soft voice and his heartbeat right up against {{user}}’s other ear makes it hard for them to hear what’s going on. He sounds flustered, with his words tumbling out in a hurry.
